item.title: I WANT MY MOMMY!
item.content:encoded:
I wonder what context that was from…
if only I were good at writing tales.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image is under an All Rights Reserved license, which is incompatible with the site's license. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is clear that cosmetic and superficial damage to the exterior is lasting. As such, experiments must adhere to a guideline that they do not cause further damage to this area.

There should be something in the containment procedure about this. Maybe delete the second sentence from this quote and add, "Care must be taken not to damage the object's exterior."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddammit, that last line. I just read Timmy because I'm going through Series II in order I cannot handle this right now thank you very much

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … holy fuck, there's a kid in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Last line caught me off guard

item.title: Great!
item.content:encoded:
This one's one of my favorite SCPs, considering my love of literature. I'd love to just sit there and share stories with the book.

Also, the last line cracks me up, and that's good.

Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sad face :( I just wanna tell it a story.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Personnel are instructed to cease any oration that is seen to provoke an unintended negative response" doesn't mean "DON'T MAKE IT ANGRY OR IT'LL BITE YOUR FINGERS". It means "if you're telling it a story and it starts to get upset, and you hadn't intended this, stop, because there's usually no reason to torment the poor thing."

If the book slams itself shut, then yes, you can (relatively) easily force it open again. But then its contents will be all "AAAAAH NO NO NO NO NO NO LEAVE ME ALONE NO NO NO I HATE YOU I HATE YOU I HATE YOU I WANT MY MOMMY AAAAAAAAAAAAA LET ME GO LET ME GO LET ME GO LET ME GO YOU UGLY SMELLY POOP-HEAD" for several hours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The bit about angering to me suggests that the warning is there as a risk against injury. If you let a decent sized textbook close by itself it probably shuts with more force than that. If it's risk of injured researcher fingers being implied then the force needs to be ramped up to a couple of thousand Newtons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm much stronger than a squirrel, but a squirrel won't allow me to handle it. If the SCP is upset with you and you try to handle it, it'll throw a tantrum. It's not very strong, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It says it can
"close itself with up to 15N of force"
meaning it shouldn't be
"physically handled…by those who had angered it previously".

Thing is, 15N isn't a whole lot of force. I'm no physicist, but I'm pretty sure just resting your hand on it would vastly overpower it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because of the very last line in the addendum, that occurred exactly one time.
I have never before wanted to give a book a comforting hug :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not too sure about this. Kerning means adjusting the negative spacing between individual characters. Bringing the letters closer to each other would give some more room on the page, but I don't think that's what he meant. A simple way to put it could be "In the event that a page is physically removed from the object, text fades from the excised page immediately and the text on the remaining pages aligns to accommodate the original full text."
But this is just quibbling quibble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't read the original but I like this rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moderate quibble:

a phenomenon similar to "word wrap" adjusts the text on all remaining pages to make room the text from the removed page.

The word you want here is "kerning". A better way to say this migh be "In the event that a page is physically removed from the object, text fades from the excised page immediately and kerning on the remaining pages is adjusted so that the original full tex is accomodated."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Point taken. Okay, so Euskara, but in the Latin alphabet, not Cyrillic.

(It was found in Portugal, which is not quite where Basque-speaking anomalous objects should be from, but it's close.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sigh, I thought of Princess Bride too with the 'no kissy' stuff, and it was done better in Princess Bride. That kid was less generic (if only barely less generic) and also wasn't the point of the story, only a framing device. (Whereas this is the center of the story.)

From where I'm standing, you need either less of that or more of it, and if more of it, then it's got to be interesting. Less generic.

Downvote cancelled, but no upvote for now.

edit: This is not to imply this was ripped from Princess Bride, of course. I'm just sayin' it's a generic Little Boy, too generic for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"A book?"
"That's right. When I was your age, television was called books. And this is a special book. It was the book my father used to read to me when I was sick, and I used to read it to your father. And today I'm gonna read it to you."
"Has it got any sports in it?"
"Are you kidding? Fencing, fighting, torture, revenge, giants, monsters, chases, escapes, true love, miracles…"

Yeah, I like this now that it's more than a Harry Potter prop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the last line that makes it for me.

Upvoting for being exactly the sort of thing I told the original author to try before posting it to the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct, thank you for putting in the effort to rewrite this. I think you've made some substantial improvements over my version, and given it a hook it was missing before, and gotten rid of a lot of the stuff I sort of shoehorned in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh yay, now it's Tom Riddles diary if he was a young child that wanted to read. I just can't find enough in this that's substantially better than the original aside from it being a kid.

Keeping my downvote because it's still a book what talks back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the rewrite, it makes me sad. It is like a little kid that is never going to grow up, struggling to find something to entertain it for the eternity is is around.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It will always initially communicate in Euskara written in the Cyrillic alphabet

This seems needlessly quirky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the re-write.
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like Tom Riddle's diary with a taste for reading. I still voted it up, because it's pretty cool, but see if you can get it fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Par. 6

though it does not become unable to be reopened

Took me a good minute to figure that one out.
I find the idea to be a wad too convoluted and the tone is stretched to much. The mechanics/properties should be explained in a single paragraph or a few smaller paragraph led together. As it is, I see a description, a bit of exposure and some expansion on the idea almost within each paragraph. It takes too long to figure out what it is (or shouldn't be).

It's sad because I can see where this could have led but it buried beneath too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, downvoted due to all the reasons listed above, but mostly because it is an overpowered book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you want to, by all means. I was expecting roughly this reception given it was my first scp and i wasn't especially confident in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't do anything for me…unless it's capable of wiping out continental Europe or the simple reading of it scares the sh*t out of me I'm not interested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty sure I can fix this. May I?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the same problem I had with it. As soon as I read that line I knew it would be a downvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a historian, questioning is what I do! God dammit, if I took the time to date every single one of my SCPs at least somewhat correctly (as in, the same century), the least the author of this can do is date it to the proper millennium.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1195 is a leather-bound book (approx. 25cm x 17cm) of indeterminate but unquestionably ancient origin

You cannot question the ancient-ness of this object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty certain no leather bound books like the one showed in the picture were made until centuries after the year 0. The book looks far too new to be as ancient as you say it is (a few hundred years, if that). Small things like that can ruin the willing suspension of disbelief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. A lot. But I'm in a minority. I'd focus on the book's personality. It's a book from the middle ages. It likes to tell people stories of heroic knights and damsels in distress. It likes to be told those stories too. You've done that but maybe bring it out more for other people to get.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I started to say just that….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but upon being responded to in any other language, it will continue to interact with the spoken/written one instead

I kind of want to respond in 1337 $P33K.

SCP-1195 appears to be sentient. It does not identify itself by any name or designation (Including SCP-1195) but appears to have a friendly, encouraging personality which enjoys "reading" new literature.

I don't believe that's sentient, but instead sapient which is kinds a one-up of sentience.

invariably results in [DATA EXPUNGED] up to ███°C, causing rapid air expansion which generates an outward force of roughly ███ Newtons

Jesus fuck what. Poor expungement, silly 'don't piss it off or else' effect.

Any stub left in the book is "pulled in", and SCP-1195 will [DATA EXPUNGED] often fatal.

Rather silly expungements here and there. You should address them and ask if you really need the expungement there.

ntil further notice, no experimentation is to take place regarding SCP-1195's interaction with other literary-themed SCPs, especially SCP-423 and SCP-826. The dangers inherent in introducing a sentient intelligence to another SCP must be approached with due caution.

We don't need this note, since excessive cross-testing is rather silly. AFAIK, we don't even do that shit anymore excepting a few, well, exceptions.

Overall, I'm with Hyaenaboy. You try to give the book too much of a personality, losing any particular interesting things you may have had from bad tone, mostly from how much description you give personificating a book. Whatever interesting things may be there are poorly expunged. If you want to improve, I'd suggest turning it around right to its core, and expounding from there. What do you want to do with the concept? Creep us out? Intrigue us? What about it is the thing that's supposed to 'get' us?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Bad expungement
  • Bad tone
  • Boring idea

I really don't know what else to say about this one. One big thing that perseveres throughout the article is the bad expungement. Your goal as a writer of an SCP is to expunge as little as absolutely possible.

(With the exception of ████████████ ██ █████)

and SCP-1195 will [DATA EXPUNGED] often fatal

Why are these here? What do they do? Case and point.

The object also gets very Mary Sue-ish: it has a personality with artistic preferences, it can talk, it can move, it can blow people up, and it can't be destroyed it's regenerative. And it has the death sentence in the Containment Procedures:

Due to the generally amicable disposition of SCP-1195…

Noting that the object has been friendly to the Foundation, especially when that personality trait makes us give it stuff, is usually a tonal death sentence for an SCP. And it makes me not like it.

And not to be one of "those" people, but this is also very long for the small amount of relevant information in the article. It feels like it diverges from scientific research into All About My Anime Character and The Cool Powers It has.

Structural stuff aside, this would be better if you focused less on the object as an entity and more on the object as an object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It never is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it wasn't just me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We had something like this a while back, it even knew how to kill 682. This on the other hand is bland, boring, and unoriginal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's…it's like that diary from Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one…. but then I have tastes from beyond Jupiter. You've got my upvote, in any case.
